title: Chiococca motleyana
intro: Chiococca motleyana, also known as the Chiococca vine, is a tropical plant species native to the Americas, belonging to the family Apocynaceae and characterized by its climbing habit and fragrant flowers. The Chiococca vine has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Chiococca motleyana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-cancer activities. In herbal medicine, the Chiococca vine is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat fever, inflammation, and skin conditions.
